creative_brief: Minimal design, warm white background, single green CTA button. No hero image — let the story breathe. Small farm texture or soil photograph as a subtle footer element only.
subject: This closes Friday and I can't hold it
preheader: 467 spots claimed. When the VIP list locks, it locks for good.
body:
Hi {first_name},

Last Thursday at 4pm, I had to call a sheep farmer named Doug outside Orange and tell him we couldn't take his lamb yet. Not because of quality — his pastures are regenerative, his animals are healthy, his price is fair.

Because we're not funded enough to say yes.

That phone call sat in my chest for two days. Doug's been farming that land for thirty-one years. He doesn't need another maybe.

This is the part most people don't see. Behind every farm-direct grocery item you'll eventually pick up from our Brookvale hub is a farmer waiting on someone to build the bridge between their paddock and your kitchen table. That's what FarmThru is. A bridge. No unnecessary middlemen. No 2,400km supply chain. Just farm to hub to you.

But bridges cost money to build.

We're raising through equity crowdfunding on Birchal — and our VIP list is how you get first access when the campaign opens. A $5 refundable deposit holds your spot. That's it. No lock-in. No obligation to invest.

Here's what I need you to know: the VIP list closes before the campaign launches. Once it's shut, there's no backdoor. No "just sneak me in." VIP members get priority access, exclusive Q&A with me, and updates before anyone else sees them.

The minimum investment is $50. The maximum is $10,000 per investor. You'd own a piece of what we're building — a grocery store where you can trace every item back to the farm it came from, order when you want with no subscription, and collect from Brookvale Monday to Friday.

I don't want to make that call to Doug again. I want to call him back and say yes.

Help me make that call.

[Claim your $5 VIP spot before the list closes →]

This is not financial advice. Consider seeking independent financial advice. Full details in the disclosure document on Birchal.

Rachel & the FarmThru team
